events.title: Iranian Drones and Missiles Strike Prince Sultan Air Base
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles targeted Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This follows earlier strikes in the month that also damaged KC-135s at the same base, indicating a sustained Iranian counterair campaign.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: Israeli Strikes on Beirut
events.desc: A large wave of Israeli strikes hit Beirut, southern Lebanon, and the eastern Bekaa Valley, causing significant destruction and casualties. The attacks occurred hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced, raising questions about the scope and adherence to the truce.

events.title: Hezbollah Rejects Direct Negotiations with Israel
events.desc: Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad stated that the group rejects direct negotiations with Israel, despite Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's announcement of intent to start peace talks. Fayyad emphasized that the Lebanese government should prioritize a ceasefire and Israeli troop withdrawal.

events.title: US Republicans Block Vote to End Iran War Powers
events.desc: US House Republicans blocked a Democratic resolution aimed at ending US attacks on Iran, preventing a vote on curbing President Trump's war powers. This action signifies continued partisan division regarding the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Remains Low Despite Ceasefire
events.desc: Maritime tracking data indicates minimal traffic through the Strait of Hormuz since the US-Iran ceasefire took effect, suggesting lingering fears and caution among shipping companies despite the announced truce.

events.title: UN Envoy Visits Tehran, Inspects Bomb Damage
events.desc: UN Secretary-General's envoy Jean Arnault met with Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister in Tehran as part of UN efforts to end the war. Arnault also visited civilian sites, including a university and apartment block, damaged by recent air strikes.

events.title: US to Host Lebanon-Israel Talks
events.desc: The US administration has informed Beirut of its plan to host separate negotiations between Lebanon and Israel. This initiative aims to address regional tensions distinct from the ongoing Iran peace talks.

events.title: Dozens of States Condemn Attacks on UN Peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.desc: A joint statement by 63 countries and the EU at the UN condemned aggressive behavior towards peacekeepers in Lebanon and expressed deep concern over the humanitarian situation, including civilian casualties and infrastructure destruction.

events.title: Lebanon Seeks Temporary Ceasefire for Broader Talks with Israel
events.desc: A senior Lebanese official stated that Lebanon is advocating for a temporary ceasefire to facilitate broader talks with Israel, envisioning a model similar to the US-Iran truce brokered by Pakistan. The US is sought as a mediator and guarantor.

events.title: Iranians Rally to Honor Slain Supreme Leader Khamenei
events.desc: Thousands of Iranians held rallies nationwide to honor Ali Khamenei, the former supreme leader killed in the early days of the US-Israeli conflict. The events occurred during the traditional 40-day mourning period and voiced criticism of potential peace talks.

events.title: Israel to Hold Direct Peace Talks with Lebanon
events.desc: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel will begin direct peace negotiations with Lebanon, focusing on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations. This announcement follows a significant wave of Israeli strikes across Lebanon.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Violate Ceasefire, Iran Claims
events.desc: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister stated that recent Israeli strikes in Lebanon constitute a 'grave violation' of the US-Iran ceasefire and demanded the US choose between continuing the war or upholding the truce.

events.title: US-Iran Ceasefire: Uncertainty Remains
events.desc: Despite the announced two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran, the situation remains fragile. Negotiators face significant challenges in reconciling the US's 15-point plan with Iran's 10 proposals to achieve a lasting peace.

events.title: US Journalist Kidnapped in Iraq Released
events.desc: US journalist Shelly Kittleson, who was reportedly kidnapped in Iraq, has been released. Officials are currently supporting her safe departure from the country.

events.title: French Couple Released After Imprisonment in Iran
events.desc: French citizens Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris have been permitted to leave Iran after being held in the country for over three years. Their release was confirmed by the French president.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been leaked online. The breach affects ministries involved in sensitive work, exposing basic security vulnerabilities.

events.title: Iran War Impacts Expand Across Middle East and North Africa
events.desc: The war with Iran is having widespread effects beyond direct combat zones, impacting Gulf Arab states, Iraq, Jordan, and Lebanon. The conflict highlights the long-standing and far-reaching consequences for regional stability.

events.title: Scale of Beirut Destruction Revealed
events.desc: The extent of destruction in Beirut following a recent wave of Israeli strikes is becoming apparent. Significant damage has been inflicted on the city, with casualty figures mounting.

events.title: Iran Warns Against Diplomacy Undermining
events.desc: Iran's Abbas Araghchi issued a warning, implying it would be unwise for the US to allow Israel's actions in Lebanon to obstruct diplomatic efforts, suggesting potential repercussions.

events.title: IMF Warns of Looming Inflation Crisis
events.desc: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has issued a warning of an impending inflation crisis, attributing the risk to the US-Israel war on Iran, which has driven up oil and gas prices and damaged energy infrastructure.

events.title: US Democrats Urge Ceasefire Inclusion of Lebanon
events.desc: Democratic lawmakers have advised the White House that any ceasefire agreement with Iran must explicitly include Lebanon. They expressed concern that continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon could reignite the broader regional conflict.

events.title: Sidon Residents Recall Destruction from Israeli Strikes
events.desc: Residents in Sidon are assessing the devastation left by Israeli strikes that flattened a religious complex. The attacks occurred despite the announcement of a US-Iran ceasefire, causing further destruction and distress.

events.title: US Republicans Block Bid to Rein in Trump Iran War Powers
events.desc: House Republicans on Thursday blocked a Democratic attempt to end U.S. attacks on Iran, continuing to prevent efforts to limit President Trump's war powers. A 'pro forma' session was ended before Democrats could propose the resolution.

events.title: UK PM Starmer: Iran Conflict a Turning Point
events.desc: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated that the Iran war must serve as a turning point for the UK, promising to bolster the economy and military to navigate a more volatile global landscape. The ceasefire announced by the US has not led to Iran lifting its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Israel Tensions Surface Amidst Democratic Midterm Planning
events.desc: Democrats are experiencing internal clashes regarding the influence of pro-Israel lobbying and Israel's military actions. Resolutions to recognize a Palestinian state and restrict military aid to Israel are being considered ahead of the midterm elections.

events.title: Pakistan's High-Stakes Iran Peace Bid
events.desc: Pakistan faces significant pressure to broker a peace deal between Iran and the United States. Diplomats view this mission as extremely challenging, given the ongoing fragile truce and the potential for instability along Pakistan's borders.

events.title: Israel Launches Deadly Blitz on Lebanon Post-Ceasefire
events.desc: Israel conducted a massive wave of strikes across Lebanon, killing hundreds, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced. Reports indicate that Lebanon was not included in the US-brokered truce, leading to continued violence.

events.title: BBC at Site of Israeli Air Strikes in Beirut
events.desc: Images and reports from the scene of Israeli air strikes in Beirut reveal extensive damage, with Lebanese health officials reporting a high number of fatalities and injuries.

events.title: US War Objectives in Iran Questioned
events.desc: The article examines whether the US has achieved its primary war objectives in Iran, which initially focused on preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons and degrading its military arsenal.

events.title: Enduring Damage to Iranian Children from War
events.desc: The long-term psychological impact on children in Iran, stemming from the ongoing conflict, is highlighted. Testimonies from parents and aid workers underscore the profound distress caused by the war, even if fighting ceases.

events.title: Oil Prices Plunge, Shares Rise on US-Iran Ceasefire Plan
events.desc: The conditional ceasefire between the US and Iran has led to a significant drop in crude oil prices, with shares experiencing a rise. However, oil prices remain considerably higher than pre-war levels.

events.title: Israel Backs Ceasefire, Netanyahu's Goals Unmet
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has responded cautiously to the US-Iran ceasefire, with his administration's war objectives remaining unfulfilled. His muted response contrasts with the more positive statements from the US and Iran.

events.title: Ceasefire Deal Settles Hardliners in Iran
events.desc: The two-week truce between the US and Iran has created unease among hardliners in Iran, despite opening the possibility of direct talks with the US. This internal division is analyzed as a consequence of the deal.

events.title: US-Iran Ceasefire: Trump's Exit Strategy at a High Cost
events.desc: The recently established two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran may offer President Trump a way to disengage from the conflict, but it comes at a significant cost, potentially altering global perceptions of the United States.

events.title: What We Know About the US-Iran Ceasefire
events.desc: A provisional truce has been agreed upon between the US and Iran, lasting two weeks. This development comes over a month after coordinated attacks were launched by the US and Israel against Iran.

events.title: Turkey Launches First Overseas Drilling Mission in Somalia
events.desc: Turkey has initiated its first overseas drilling mission in Somalia with a dedicated drilling ship, amid escalating regional rivalries in the Horn of Africa.

events.title: Israel Announces Direct Talks with Lebanon
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel will commence direct peace talks with Lebanon, aiming to disarm Hezbollah and establish peaceful relations. This follows a large wave of Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

events.title: WHO Suspends Gaza Medical Evacuations After Contractor Killed
events.desc: The World Health Organization (WHO) has halted medical evacuations in Gaza after an Israeli military operation resulted in the death of a contracted individual. Israel claims troops fired on the vehicle due to perceived threats.

events.title: US to Host Lebanon-Israel Talks Next Week
events.desc: A senior Lebanese official confirmed that the Trump administration plans to host separate talks between Lebanon and Israel next week. This initiative is distinct from the ongoing peace negotiations with Iran.

events.title: Gunman Killed, Two Injured in Shooting at Istanbul Israeli Consulate
events.desc: One gunman was killed and two others were injured in a shooting incident at the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. The consulate has reportedly been empty of Israeli diplomats for the past two and a half years.

events.title: Iran's Anti-Access/Area Denial Strategy Detailed
events.desc: The article analyzes Iran's anti-access and area denial (A2/AD) strategy, contrasting its cruder but still dangerous approach with China's. It highlights the immediate threat faced upon approaching Iranian operational areas.

events.title: US Fertility Rate Hits Record Low
events.desc: The United States fertility rate has fallen to an all-time low, continuing a two-decade decline. Since 2007, the rate has dropped by nearly 23 percent.

events.title: Hezbollah a Sticking Point in Iran Ceasefire Talks
events.desc: The Lebanese armed group Hezbollah remains a significant factor in the discussions surrounding a potential Iran ceasefire, despite being an ally of Tehran and currently under attack from Israel.

events.title: US Foreign Aid Declined Significantly in 2025
events.desc: The OECD reports a historic decline in US foreign aid in 2025, attributed to cuts made by the Trump administration. This decrease occurred while humanitarian needs globally continue to grow.

events.title: Iran-US Ceasefire Deal Creates Diplomatic Opportunities for Pakistan
events.desc: The US-Iran truce is seen as beneficial for Pakistan, enhancing its mediation role and strengthening a regional balance favorable to China's allies. This development boosts Pakistan's strategic position.

events.title: Greenland Responds to Trump's 'Ice' Insult
events.desc: Greenland's Prime Minister criticized President Trump's recent remarks, asserting Greenland's status as a proud nation committed to maintaining global order, in response to his dismissive comment calling it a 'piece of ice'.

events.title: UK Tracks Russian Submarines During Covert Operation
events.desc: The UK defense minister reported that three Russian submarines were tracked during a covert operation. The operation did not find evidence of Russia damaging undersea infrastructure.

events.title: Mayor Mamdani Reflects on First 100 Days
events.desc: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani discussed his first 100 days in office, covering issues such as childcare, housing, bigotry, Iran, and the future of the Democratic Party. Public reviews of his record were also solicited.

events.title: LA28 Olympic Ticket Sales Open Globally Amidst Record Demand
events.desc: Ticket sales for the LA28 Olympic Games have opened worldwide following record-breaking domestic demand. More tickets were sold to the local audience than for any previous Olympic Games.

events.title: Al Jazeera Condemns Killing of Journalist in Gaza
events.desc: Al Jazeera has condemned the killing of journalist Mohammed Wishah in Gaza, following an Israeli strike. The Israeli military claims Wishah was a 'Hamas terrorist,' a designation previously denied by the Qatar-based network.

events.title: Iranian School Strike Details Revealed via New Videos
events.desc: Bellingcat has verified new videos showing two distinct waves of missiles hitting an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran, in late February. The videos were released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

events.title: Evidence Suggests US Scattered Mines Over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence indicates the US deployed Gator Scatterable Mine systems over the village of Kafari in southern Iran, resulting in casualties. Experts identified the munitions as US-made Gator anti-tank mines.

events.title: Chad Strike Munition Remnants Match RSF Weapon
events.desc: Munition remnants from a deadly strike in Tiné, Chad, appear to match a weapon previously used by Sudan's Rapid Support Forces (RSF), despite RSF denials of involvement. The strike killed at least 17 people.

events.title: Indian Ruling Party Using AI to Amplify Hate Speech
events.desc: India's ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is reportedly using AI to generate and amplify hate speech, particularly in regions near Bangladesh. Videos posted by a BJP state branch show targeted messaging against minority groups.

events.title: UAE Downplaying Iranian Strikes
events.desc: Authorities in the United Arab Emirates have reportedly downplayed damage and misrepresented interceptions of Iranian drone strikes. Official statements often differ from open-source observations, potentially rewriting the narrative of the conflict.

events.title: Croatia Football Team Sponsor Controversy
events.desc: Croatia's national football team has signed a deal with a gambling sponsor whose representative allegedly used a false name. The sponsor is described as an opaque Asian-facing bookmaker, raising concerns ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Iran War's Impact on US Strategic Thinking
events.desc: The war with Iran has exposed a decline in the US's grasp of 'strategic time,' characterized by bureaucratic cuts and incentives favoring immediacy over long-term statecraft. The dismantling of the Pentagon's Office of Net Assessment is cited as an example.

events.title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz and Pilot Rescue Missions
events.desc: Retired admirals discuss the military challenges of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, detailing how Iran throttles navigation. Strategies explored include mines, drones, missiles, and potential raids on Kharg Island, in addition to air strikes.

events.title: China's Tech Companies as Military Suppliers
events.desc: A hidden system integrates Chinese tech companies, including robot manufacturers like Unitree Robotics, into the military supply chain. Despite pledges against weaponization, the dual-use nature of their technology raises concerns about military applications.

events.title: Closing Air and Missile Defense Gaps in Indo-Pacific
events.desc: The recent conflict highlighted significant US expenditure on missile interceptors to support Israeli air and missile defense. The article examines the need to address gaps in air and missile defense capabilities within the Indo-Pacific region.

events.title: Precision Weaponry Reengineered Modern Warfare
events.desc: An interview discusses the historical development and impact of Paveway, a precision-guided bomb, on air campaigns and modern warfare. It explores the relationship between government, science, and industry in weapon development.

events.title: Ukraine Lessons on Army Failures: The Breach Point
events.desc: The article analyzes why armies fail, using the Ukrainian conflict as a case study. It emphasizes that breaching and gap-crossing capacity are critical for land forces against prepared defenses, citing the failure to advance at the Novodarivka breach point.

events.title: NATO Air Defense Future Unfolding Amid Tensions
events.desc: Revisiting an earlier analysis, this article examines the evolution of NATO's air and missile defense capabilities amidst alliance tensions and ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

events.title: US Republicans Block Bid to Rein in Trump Iran War Powers
events.desc: House Republicans blocked a Democratic attempt to end U.S. attacks on Iran, preventing efforts to rein in President Trump's war powers. The procedural move occurred during a 'pro forma' House session.

events.title: UK: Iran Conflict Requires New Path
events.desc: Prime Minister Keir Starmer declared that the Iran war necessitates a new direction for Britain, promising economic and military strengthening to address a more dangerous world. The conflict's disruption to energy supplies via the Strait of Hormuz remains critical.

events.title: Israel Tensions Surface Amidst Democratic Midterm Planning
events.desc: Democrats are clashing over the influence of pro-Israel lobbying and Israel's military actions. Resolutions on recognizing a Palestinian state and restricting aid to Israel are being debated at a key strategy conference.

events.title: Hezbollah Rejects Direct Talks with Israel
events.desc: Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad stated the group's rejection of direct negotiations with Israel, emphasizing that the Lebanese government should prioritize a ceasefire before any further steps are taken.

events.title: UN Envoy Meets Iran Deputy Minister, Visits Bomb Sites
events.desc: UN envoy Jean Arnault met Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister in Tehran as part of UN efforts for peace. He also visited civilian sites damaged by recent air strikes.

events.title: Analysis: Pakistan's Risky Iran Peace Bid
events.desc: Pakistan is under immense pressure to broker a peace deal between Iran and the United States, a task deemed potentially impossible by some diplomats due to the fragile truce and regional instability.

events.title: US to Host Lebanon-Israel Talks Next Week
events.desc: The US administration has informed Beirut of its plan to host separate negotiations between Lebanon and Israel next week, a move aimed at addressing regional tensions apart from the ongoing Iran peace talks.

events.title: Dozens of States Condemn Attacks on UN Peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.desc: A joint statement by 63 countries and the EU at the UN condemned 'unacceptable aggressive behavior' towards UN peacekeepers in Lebanon and expressed deep concern over the humanitarian situation.

events.title: Lebanon Seeks Temporary Ceasefire for Broader Talks with Israel
events.desc: Lebanon is advocating for a temporary ceasefire to facilitate broader talks with Israel, proposing a model similar to the US-Iran truce. The US is sought as a mediator and guarantor for any agreement.

events.title: Iranians Pay Tribute to Slain Supreme Leader Khamenei
events.desc: Thousands of Iranians rallied to honor Ali Khamenei, the former supreme leader killed on the war's first day. Participants criticized the upcoming peace talks in Pakistan.

events.title: China Backs US-Iran Ceasefire, Boosts Pakistan's Role
events.desc: China's support for the US-Iran truce is seen as advantageous for Pakistan, enhancing its mediation role and strengthening a regional balance that favors Beijing's allies.

events.title: Netanyahu: Israel Wants Peace Talks with Lebanon ASAP
events.desc: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated Israel's desire to commence peace talks with Lebanon as soon as possible, focusing on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Called 'Grave Violation' of Ceasefire
events.desc: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister told the BBC that Israeli strikes in Lebanon are a 'grave violation' of the US-Iran ceasefire and urged the US to choose between war and peace.

events.title: Ships Cautious Approaching Strait of Hormuz Amid Fragile Ceasefire
events.desc: Analysis shows only a few vessels have traversed the Strait of Hormuz since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, indicating continued apprehension among ships despite the fragile truce.

events.title: Bowen: Ceasefire Offers Respite, But Longevity Uncertain
events.desc: International Editor Jeremy Bowen writes that while the ceasefire provides a respite for civilians, its sustainability is questionable. The ongoing war and its consequences are reshaping the Middle East.

events.title: At Least 182 Killed Across Lebanon in Israeli Strikes
events.desc: A large wave of Israeli strikes targeted Beirut suburbs, southern Lebanon, and the Bekaa Valley, resulting in at least 182 deaths and 890 injuries, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The attacks occurred hours after a US-Iran ceasefire.

events.title: French Couple Released After Over Three Years in Iranian Jail
events.desc: French citizens Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris have been allowed to leave Iran after more than three years of detention. Their departure was confirmed by the French president.

infrastructure.name: Prince Sultan Air Base
infrastructure.note: Royal Saudi Air Force and US Air Force base
